title: submit-flow input spec
description:
Spec passed to ``hpc-mapreduce submit-flow --spec <file>``.

Workflow atom that does pre-flight + rsync + deploy + optional
canary + qsub + record in one shot. All judgment (which
constraint, which walltime, which executor, scaffold tasks.py)
is the caller's responsibility — this atom takes resolved values
and executes.

script.description: Path to the job script ON THE CLUSTER (e.g. .hpc/templates/cpu_array.sh). deploy_runtime places these under remote_path/.hpc/templates/.
script.title: Script

job_env.description: Env vars forwarded into the cluster job. Caller is responsible for setting EXECUTOR, HPC_RUN_ID, HPC_CMD_SHA, HPC_TASK_COUNT, REPO_DIR, MODULES, CONDA_SOURCE/CONDA_ENV (as needed), HPC_RUNTIME (if uv), HPC_CAMPAIGN_ID (if part of a campaign).
job_env.title: Job Env

pass_env_keys.description: SGE-only: which job_env keys to forward via qsub -v. Null = forward every key in job_env. Ignored for SLURM (slurm forwards everything in job_env automatically via --export ALL,...).
pass_env_keys.title: Pass Env Keys

slurm_account.description: SLURM-only: account to charge (sbatch --account). Defaults to whatever SLURM picks for the user.
slurm_account.title: Slurm Account

slurm_cluster.description: SLURM-only: cluster name for federated SLURM installations (sbatch --clusters=). Most installs don't need this.
slurm_cluster.title: Slurm Cluster

tasks_per_array.description: Batch tasks into arrays of this size. Null = single array of total_tasks.
tasks_per_array.title: Tasks Per Array

canary.description: Submit a 1-task canary first; abort if it fails. Skip when caller has just smoke-tested or knows the pipeline is good.
canary.title: Canary

skip_preflight.description: Skip the pre-flight check (caller has just run it; saves one SSH probe). Use with caution.
skip_preflight.title: Skip Preflight

partial_ok.description: When true, the sidecar records partial_ok=True under the `extra` pocket. monitor-flow consults this on terminal classification: a wave with at least one success is reported as `complete` (instead of `failed`) when partial_ok=true, and a `<run_id>.failed.json` ledger lists the failed task IDs. aggregate-flow honors the same ledger by skipping those task IDs and reporting `partial_failures`.
partial_ok.title: Partial Ok
